chapters.title: Joe Biden caught the political bug at twelve
chapters.text:
Joe Biden's first taste of politics came at twelve, in an unexpected setting, his grandfather's kitchen.

The family home was in Delaware, but his grandparents lived in Scranton, Pennsylvania, and the Bidens visited them most weekends. Joe loved those weekends. He ran around the neighborhood with friends, ate big meals, and soaked up the family feel.

The Bidens were Irish Catholics, just like everyone else on the block. Every Sunday they went to mass, and afterward they gathered for a huge meal. That was where Joe first learned about politics, listening to the men debate in the kitchen, getting loud and animated as they argued their views.

From an early age, Joe Biden understood that politics is a topic that stirs strong feelings.

His grandfather would explain what was happening in the world and how the government was handling it. Right there, Joe learned that no political group should be considered better or more important than another. From his grandfather, he understood that politics is about giving your word and keeping it. That lesson has stayed with him ever since.

In his own words, he believes, as his grandpop did, that his chosen profession is a noble calling.

chapters.title: A stutter was never going to hold him back
chapters.text:
Joe Biden had a stutter in his early years, but he refused to let it interfere with his schoolwork, social life, or whatever he dreamed up next. Still, he felt embarrassed and worried in situations he had not prepared for, like public speaking.

Joe Biden never let his speech impediment stop him from aiming for the top. He figured out how to manage it and eventually beat it.

He learned to control the stutter by predicting what would come next, so he could get ready in his head. If he knew he would have to read aloud in class, he would rehearse in advance. Despite the difficulties the stutter caused, he does not look back and wish it had never happened. He knows it shaped who he is and taught him important lessons about life and persistence.

After switching schools in his early teens, Joe was asked to read in front of the class by one of the nuns at his Catholic school. He was caught off guard and started to stutter. Instead of encouraging him or letting it pass, the nun made fun of him in front of everyone. Joe was angry and embarrassed, and he stood up and stormed out of the classroom. The school called his mother, who asked him what had happened. When she heard the truth, she went straight to the school and warned the nun and the headteacher never to do that again.

Joe speaks warmly of his parents. Although his mother was more outspoken about values and what mattered, he always kept his word and showed strong resolve. Joe has used this as inspiration throughout his career.

He still draws on the strong values of his mother and father, both for his own life and in raising his children.

chapters.title: He met the love of his life on a beach trip
chapters.text:
In 1961, Joe Biden started studying at the University of Delaware, majoring in history and political science, with plans to go on to law school. His first college years were not the most studious. He got caught up playing college football and chasing girls. Then a spring break trip to the Bahamas changed his life for good.

On the beach, Joe and his friends spotted two girls, one blonde and one brunette. They playfully argued about who would talk to whom, and Joe decided to approach the blonde. A few years later, he ended up marrying her.

Joe met his first wife, Neilia, in 1961 on that spring break trip. He fell in love at first sight, which is proof that it really does happen.

Joe and Neilia Hunter fell for each other almost instantly, and he told her on that trip that she was the one he would marry. From then on, he visited her in New York every weekend, scraping together money however he could to fund the trips. His grades also improved because he was focused on graduating and being closer to Neilia.

After graduation, he applied to a law school in Syracuse, where Neilia was teaching after her own graduation. Tuition was steep, though, and he realized he would need a job to pay for his education.

chapters.title: He found his calling in the public defenders office
chapters.text:
Joe Biden applied for several jobs, but his lack of credentials made it tough. He got a break at a law firm close to Neilia's hometown of Wilmington. He learned a lot in a short time, but the job did not last. From there, he chose to work as a public defender in Wilmington.

Joe chose the public defenders office after seeing the racial injustice many residents faced, especially in Wilmington. Many people could not afford a lawyer, and a historic ruling five years earlier had established that anyone headed to court would be assigned a public defender if they could not hire one.

Joe has been passionate about racial injustice throughout his career. It goes back to his time in Wilmington.

He admits it was not an easy role, but he found satisfaction in helping people who had no other way to help themselves. That theme has run through his political career and into his presidency. Joe is known for his empathy, and working in the public defenders office showed him that many people struggle, sometimes through no fault of their own.

chapters.title: Family and a fast-rising career arrived at once
chapters.text:
In February 1968, Joe and Neilia's first son, Beau, was born, and three months later, Neilia found out she was expecting again. Hunter, their second son, was born in February 1969. The couple started house hunting to find a place big enough for their young family. They eventually found a house a short distance from Maryland and began renovating it to suit their taste.

Most days, Joe rushed home to be with his family after work, but on Wednesday evenings he stayed a little late for the Democratic Forum meeting. At one of these meetings, a member asked if he would consider running for county council in New Castle. Despite some hesitation, Biden won the election in November 1970 and got to work in his new role.

Biden had a family-first approach from very early in his career. His family has always backed him.

As more money slowly came into the Biden household, the couple started buying furniture. They wanted pieces that would last. Things improved further in 1971, when he was asked to run for the Senate.

At first, Biden did not think he stood a chance, since he was not even thirty. Neilia had also just given birth to their third child, Naomi, and Biden did not want to be away from his family too much. After talking it over with his wife, he decided to go for it.

As a senator, he knew he could make a difference on the issues that mattered to him, like war and peace, the environment, crime, civil rights, and women's rights.

He won a seat in the Senate, and on the day his victory was announced, he told everyone they did not have to agree with him, but at least he would always be clear about where he stood. His goal was to build a large reserve of trust. He was excited about the new role and felt he could get in there and create real change.

Even at the start of his career, Biden focused on building trust. He was always open, honest, and clear about his goals.

chapters.title: One phone call changed everything
chapters.text:
Just before his first day in the Senate, Biden traveled to Washington to interview staff. Since it was close to Christmas, Neilia decided to stay home with the children and start decorating for the holiday.

Biden got a call during his work saying there had been an accident. He was told it was probably nothing serious, but he heard a slight change in tone and knew at once that it was. He knew Neilia was gone.

Neilia had been driving the car with all three children in the back when the accident happened. Neilia and the baby, Naomi, were killed at the scene, while Hunter and Beau suffered serious injuries.

He later wrote that he could not speak, only felt a hollow core growing in his chest, as if he was being sucked into a black hole.

In the days after the accident, he kept a vigil beside his sons' hospital beds. He felt completely numb. At that point, he understood why some people felt driven to end it all. But a glance at his sons forced him to keep focusing on what they needed.

In his bleakest moments, the pain was unbearable, but Joe focused on being there for his boys.

Two weeks after the accident, he was due to be sworn into the Senate, but it was the last thing on his mind. Senators kept calling, urging him not to give up, but he was not listening at first. In the end, he told them to give him six months. He knew Neilia had pushed him hard to earn his place in the Senate and that she would not want him to stop now. He did it for her.

After John F. Kennedy, Joe Biden is the second Catholic president of the United States.

chapters.title: Love came back into his life when he least expected it
chapters.text:
After a successful first term in the Senate, Biden was starting to think about running again. The boys had settled into a routine and were doing well. That was when he met a woman who would change his life and make him feel whole again, Jill Jacobs.

Jill was a strong woman with no interest in having anything to do with a senator. Biden was also a decade older than her and a single father of two sons. As they dated, Biden took things slowly, waiting to introduce her to Hunter and Beau, who were six and seven at the time. He need not have worried, because both boys took to Jill quickly and told their father he should marry her.

Biden met his second wife, Jill, and instantly felt that Neilia had sent her into his life. They married in 1977.

Joe and Jill were married in New York City in 1977. The ceremony was small, with only family attending, and both boys stood with the couple at the altar. Jill slipped into the family almost seamlessly, and both boys took to her as their mom very quickly. Biden often thought that Neilia had sent Jill into their lives, and Jill always did whatever she could to honor Neilia's memory and keep her a part of the home.

chapters.title: A plagiarism scandal brought the wolves to his door
chapters.text:
Biden's personal life was settling down, and he could focus on his career with the support of his wife and children. In 1988, he decided to put together a run for president, but it quickly fell apart over allegations of plagiarism during a speech.

During his 1988 presidential bid, Biden was accused of plagiarism, and relentless news coverage forced him to withdraw from the race.

Parts of Biden's speech appeared to have been taken from a speech by British politician Neil Kinnock. Biden maintained that he had credited Kinnock in the speech, but the journalists chasing the story were quick to point out that he had not. Many felt the opposition was building a case to question Biden's integrity, the one thing he held dear throughout his political career. It often felt like the sharks were circling.

Biden was advised to apologize, but he did not want to, because he felt he had done nothing wrong. The longer he stayed silent, the more stories piled up. In the end, he pulled out of the presidential race, understanding that the allegations were not going to disappear and that the White House was pushing them from behind the scenes.

Biden was devastated to withdraw. He felt the White House had driven the agenda.

chapters.title: Death came calling at his door once more
chapters.text:
Much of Biden's professional life has been shaped by events in his personal life. From the death of his wife and child, to remarrying, to the controversy of his ill-fated presidential bid, Biden was dealt another cruel blow. This time it was his health.

After withdrawing from the presidential race, Biden started getting headaches that slowly got worse. He thought he had simply pinched a nerve while working out, and a doctor agreed after noticing some numbness in his right hand. The pain did not improve, and he ended up in the hospital after a particularly bad episode.

After surviving brain surgery with only a fifty-fifty chance of success, Biden came to appreciate the small things in life.

Doctors took a spinal tap and found blood, which pointed to an aneurysm somewhere in his body. Jill arrived at the hospital just in time to hear the priest reading Biden his last rites. He needed surgery, and the odds of survival were fifty-fifty. The craniotomy had to happen at a different hospital, and he had to be transferred, with Jill holding his hand. She was there when he woke up.

Despite the joy of surviving the surgery, he ended up needing a second operation while also developing a blood clot in his lung from being inactive in bed. After doing some family research, he found that his grandparents had also had histories of aneurysms.

Throughout his time in the hospital, Jill was a rock and always there whenever he opened his eyes.

After being discharged following his second surgery, he jokingly told reporters he was going to run for president again. When he returned to the Senate after recovery, he received a standing ovation. Biden was always warmly welcomed and loved in any job he held, and that remains true today.

chapters.title: Final thoughts
chapters.text:
Joe Biden's private life has often overshadowed his professional life. Having gone through more tragedy and hardship than many people face in a lifetime, Biden has shown remarkable resilience and strength to overcome whatever life has thrown at him.

From overcoming a stutter to landing a job at a prestigious law firm with no credentials, from meeting the love of his life to losing her and their baby daughter a few years later, Biden has come through it all with grace and humility. These experiences shaped his character and made him the leader he is today.

Biden's years before becoming vice president to Barack Obama show why he was chosen for the role and perhaps why he became the forty-sixth president of the United States. Political allegiances aside, you cannot help but tip your hat and say this man deserves it.

A few habits to try. Life throws all kinds of negative situations your way. Focus on something you can control instead of getting swept away in negativity. A professional life is nothing without a strong and happy personal foundation. Do not let your job take you away from those you love. Work to maintain a strong balance between home and work. If something is holding you back, perhaps like Biden's early stutter, work out how you can control it and overcome it. Be one step ahead of the game and learn to manage it, rather than letting it manage you.
